UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information, excluding sensitive authentication data, for managing personal details, orders, and submissions within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and linking with an external authentication system.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Stores address details for shipping and billing, reusable across user profiles and orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this address belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

street1.description: The first line of the street address.

street2.description: The second line of the street address, if applicable.

stateProvince.description: The state or province of the address.

postalCode.description: The postal code or ZIP code of the address.

Brand.title: Brand
Brand.description: Represents a brand or manufacturer of products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Brand entity.

name.description: The official name of the brand.

description.description: A brief description of the brand.

logoUrl.description: URL to the brand's logo image.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines categories for products, enabling filtering and organization (e.g., 'Dresses', 'Boots', 'Accessories', 'Minimalist Style').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

parentCategoryId.description: Reference to a parent category for hierarchical categorization. (Relationship: Category 1:N Category - self-referencing)

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a distinct product in the catalog, encompassing general details and references to its specific variants.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

brandId.description: Reference to the brand that manufactures this product. (Relationship: Brand 1:N Product)

categoryIds.description: References to categories this product belongs to, supporting filtering by style, type, etc. (Relationship: Category N:N Product)

basePrice.description: The base price of the product, before any variant-specific adjustments.

primaryImageUrl.description: URL to the primary image for the product.

additionalImageUrls.description: An array of URLs to additional images for the product.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the product, often used in product page URLs.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product information was last updated.

isFeatured.description: Indicates if the product should be featured on the homepage or in special carousels.

ProductVariant.title: ProductVariant
ProductVariant.description: Represents a specific combination of attributes (like size and color) for a product, essential for inventory and exact pricing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductVariant entity.

productId.description: Reference to the parent product this variant belongs to. (Relationship: Product 1:N ProductVariant)

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for this specific variant.

color.description: The color of this product variant.

size.description: The size of this product variant.

priceAdjustment.description: An optional adjustment to the base product price for this specific variant.

stockQuantity.description: The current quantity of this variant available in stock.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image specific to this variant (e.g., showing the specific color).

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a user's shopping cart, holding products selected for potential purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this cart. Null for guest carts. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Cart)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was last updated.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp when a guest cart will expire and be emptied.

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a single product variant and its quantity within a shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the parent cart this item belongs to. (Relationship: Cart 1:N CartItem)

productVariantId.description: Reference to the specific product variant added to the cart. (Relationship: ProductVariant 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product variant in the cart.

priceAtAddToCart.description: The price of the product variant at the time it was added to the cart, to handle price changes.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a completed customer purchase, including details about the transaction, shipping, and billing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed the order. Null for guest checkouts. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including shipping and discounts.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'processed', 'shipped', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

shippingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address used for shipping. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

billingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address used for billing. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

paymentIntentId.description: Identifier from the external payment gateway (e.g., Stripe) for this transaction.

guestEmail.description: The email provided during guest checkout, if userId is null.

discountCodeUsed.description: The discount code applied to this order, if any.

discountAmount.description: The total monetary amount discounted from the order.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product variant and its quantity within a completed order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the parent order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productVariantId.description: Reference to the specific product variant included in the order. (Relationship: ProductVariant 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product variant in the order.

pricePerUnit.description: The price of the product variant at the time of the order.

productName.description: Denormalized name of the product for easier reporting and historical data.

variantDetails.description: Denormalized details of the variant (e.g., 'Color: Red, Size: M') for easier reporting.

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Stores site-wide announcements, promotions, or special offers displayed in the announcement bar.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Announcement entity.

message.description: The content of the announcement message.

startDate.description: The date and time when the announcement becomes active.

endDate.description: The date and time when the announcement should no longer be displayed.

isActive.description: Indicates if the announcement is currently active and visible.

linkUrl.description: An optional URL that the announcement can link to.

linkText.description: The text for the optional link, if a linkUrl is provided.

HeroSlide.title: HeroSlide
HeroSlide.description: Manages content for the dynamic hero section on the homepage, which can feature images or videos.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HeroSlide entity.

title.description: The main title displayed on the hero slide.

subtitle.description: A secondary subtitle or description for the hero slide.

contentType.description: Specifies the type of media for the slide (e.g., 'image', 'video').

mediaUrl.description: The URL to the image or video file for the hero slide.

altText.description: Alternative text for image content, important for accessibility.

callToActionText.description: The text for the call-to-action button on the slide.

callToActionLink.description: The URL that the call-to-action button links to.

displayOrder.description: The numerical order in which this slide should appear in the hero section.

isActive.description: Indicates if the hero slide is currently active and visible on the homepage.

Subscriber.title: Subscriber
Subscriber.description: Records user email addresses for newsletters and exclusive offers via the email capture form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscriber entity.

email.description: The email address of the subscriber.

optInDate.description: Timestamp when the user opted into the newsletter.

isSubscribed.description: Indicates if the user is currently subscribed to the newsletter.

SocialPost.title: SocialPost
SocialPost.description: Represents user-generated content from social media (e.g., Instagram), used for shoppable feeds and 'The Movement in Action' section.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SocialPost entity.

platform.description: The social media platform where the post originated (e.g., 'Instagram', 'TikTok').

externalPostId.description: The unique identifier of the post on its original social media platform.

caption.description: The text caption associated with the social media post.

mediaUrl.description: URL to the image or video media of the social post.

postLink.description: URL to the original social media post.

authorHandle.description: The social media handle or username of the post's author.

productIds.description: References to products featured in this social post, enabling shoppable integration. (Relationship: Product N:N SocialPost)

displayOrder.description: The numerical order for displaying this post in carousels or feeds.

isApproved.description: Indicates if the social post has been approved for display on the website.

postedDate.description: The date and time when the post was originally published on social media.

StorySubmission.title: StorySubmission
StorySubmission.description: Stores user-submitted stories and media for the 'Motivational Mondays' campaign, subject to moderation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StorySubmission entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who submitted the story. Null if submitted as a guest. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N StorySubmission)

guestEmail.description: Email provided by a guest user for their submission, if userId is null.

title.description: The title of the submitted story.

storyContent.description: The main text content of the user's story.

mediaUrls.description: An array of URLs to images or videos submitted with the story.

submissionDate.description: Timestamp when the story was submitted.

status.description: The current moderation status of the submission (e.g., 'pending', 'approved', 'rejected').

moderatorNotes.description: Internal notes from moderators regarding the submission.

featuredDate.description: The date and time when the story was approved and featured on the site.

Discount.title: Discount
Discount.description: Defines discount codes or promotions, including those generated by the 'Spin to Win' gamification feature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Discount entity.

code.description: The actual coupon code users can apply for the discount.

value.description: The value of the discount (e.g., 15 for 15% off, 10 for $10 off).

minOrderAmount.description: The minimum order amount required to apply this discount.

maxDiscountAmount.description: The maximum monetary value for percentage-based discounts.

usageLimitPerCoupon.description: The total number of times this specific discount code can be used across all users.

usageLimitPerUser.description: The maximum number of times a single user can apply this discount code.

usedCount.description: The current total number of times this discount code has been used.

startDate.description: The date and time when the discount becomes active.

expirationDate.description: The date and time when the discount expires.

isActive.description: Indicates if the discount is currently active and usable.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is strictly restricted to the owner where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`.

Stores shipping and billing addresses for a specific user. The `userId` is denormalized into the document (as per schema) for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches `userId` in the document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the address.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique ID of the address.

definition.description: Stores a user's shopping cart. Access is restricted to the owner where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. The `userId` field in the document must match the `userId` in the path. This structure ensures strong authorization and QAPs for user-owned carts.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the cart.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique ID of the cart.

definition.description: Individual items within a user's shopping cart. `cartId` is denormalized into the document (as per schema). For robust authorization independence from the parent `Cart`, the `userId` (owner of the cart) should also be denormalized into each `CartItem` document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the cart.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent cart.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the cart item.

definition.description: Stores completed orders for a user. Access is restricted to the owner where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. The `userId` field in the document must match the `userId` in the path. This structure ensures strong authorization and QAPs for user-owned orders.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who placed the order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the order.

definition.description: Individual items within a user's order. `orderId` is denormalized into the document (as per schema). For robust authorization independence from the parent `Order`, the `userId` (owner of the order) should also be denormalized into each `OrderItem` document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who placed the order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the order item.

definition.description: Stores user-submitted stories for 'Motivational Mondays'. Access is restricted to the owner where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`. These are private submissions awaiting moderation. `userId` is implicitly carried by the path and should be present in the document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who submitted the story.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique ID of the story submission.

definition.description: Publicly readable catalog of product brands. Write access is restricted to administrators.
Publicly readable product categories, supporting filtering. Write access is restricted to administrators.
Publicly readable product details. Write access is restricted to administrators. Supports QAPs by being a top-level collection.

Specific product variants (e.g., size, color) nested under their parent product. `productId` is denormalized into the document (as per schema) to support authorization independence. Publicly readable. Write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent product.

params.name: variantId
params.description: The unique ID of the product variant.

definition.description: Site-wide announcements. Publicly readable. Write access restricted to administrators. Supports QAPs by being a top-level collection.
Content for the homepage hero section. Publicly readable. Write access restricted to administrators. Supports QAPs by being a top-level collection.
Publicly visible, approved user-generated social media content. `isApproved` status can be used for client-side filtering. Write access restricted to administrators.
Defines discount codes. Publicly readable for active codes (filtered client-side or with rules `if resource.data.isActive == true`). Write access restricted to administrators.

Records user email addresses for newsletters. Users can create (subscribe), but cannot read or list existing subscribers. Only administrators can read/list this collection, ensuring privacy and preventing enumeration.

Publicly readable, approved, and featured 'Motivational Mondays' stories. Documents in this collection are copied from `/users/{userId}/story_submissions` once approved and selected for featuring. This ensures structural segregation for public access (QAPs). Write access restricted to administrators.

params.name: storyId
params.description: The unique ID of the featured story.

definition.description: Used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The mere existence of a document at this path signifies that the `userId` represents an administrative user. Read access is restricted to the administrator (`userId` matches `request.auth.uid`), and write access is restricted to existing administrators. (Note: The schema is linked to UserProfile for type consistency, but only the `uid` of the document is checked for role existence.)

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who has administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for VogueVerse is designed prioritizing secure, scalable, and debuggable security rules, adhering strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This design heavily relies on denormalizing authorization context. For user-owned subcollections (e.g., user addresses, cart items, order items, story submissions), the `userId` (owner's UID) is explicitly included in the document, even when implicitly available through the path. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules to check parent document ownership, allowing for atomic operations (like adding a cart item) and greatly simplifying rule debugging. Specifically, for `CartItem` and `OrderItem`, while `userId` is not explicitly in the provided schema, it is crucial for authorization independence and must be denormalized into these documents. This ensures that access to a cart item or order item can be verified solely by inspecting its own fields and `request.auth.uid`, without needing to read the parent `Cart` or `Order` document.

**Clarity of Intent & DBAC:** Authorization intent is made explicit through hierarchical paths for user-owned data (e.g., `/users/{userId}/addresses/{addressId}`) and dedicated top-level collections for global entities. User authorization strictly relies on `request.auth.uid`. Global roles, like administrators, are managed via existence checks in a dedicated collection (`/roles_admin/{userId}`).

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** Structural segregation is used extensively to support secure `list` operations. Publicly accessible data (e.g., `Brand`, `Category`, `Product`, `Announcement`, `HeroSlide`, `SocialPost`, `Discount`) resides in top-level collections, allowing for simple `allow read: if true` rules, thereby enabling efficient and secure querying without filtering. Private user-specific data (e.g., `UserProfile`, `Address`, `Cart`, `Order`, `StorySubmission`) is strictly confined to user-specific subcollections (`/users/{userId}/...`), ensuring that `list` operations automatically scope results to the authenticated user. To handle `StorySubmission` content that transitions from private (submission) to public (featured), a dedicated `/featured_stories` collection is used, where approved stories are denormalized, maintaining a homogeneous security posture for each collection.

**Guest Data Handling:** The strict adherence to `request.auth.uid` for Firestore security rules means that direct client-side access to guest-specific data (e.g., anonymous carts, guest orders, guest story submissions) is not securely feasible via Firestore rules alone if `userId` is null. For scenarios requiring such guest data, the application would need to either: 1) immediately create an anonymous Firebase user for the guest session and use their `uid` as the `userId`, or 2) route all guest data operations through a secure backend service (e.g., Cloud Functions) which then interacts with Firestore on the guest's behalf.

This structure balances relational integrity, scalability, and security, making the application easier to develop and maintain while providing strong data protection.
